key_insight: The paper shows that stress can be computed accurately in localized-orbital DFT, including linear-scaling methods, with good convergence at modest density-matrix cutoffs.
engineering_relevance: This enables reliable stress-driven simulations such as pressure control and large-scale isothermal-isobaric molecular dynamics in computational materials design.
method: Derived stress expressions for DFT with localized orbitals.
Implemented the approach in the large-scale DFT code Conquest.
Tested stress convergence versus density-matrix range across materials with different band gaps.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports comparison of stress convergence against energy and forces, and states that large-scale isothermal-isobaric molecular dynamics was stable and accurate.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on choosing a sufficiently large density-matrix cutoff, so overly aggressive truncation may degrade stress quality.
paper_title: Stress calculation in linear scaling DFT: convergence and dynamics
